signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: Trump's announcement of 25% tariffs on European autos sets up immediate downside pressure toward the 1.1600 handle. This trade protectionism reduces European export competitiveness and weighs on the Euro, while the EUR COT net short position of -18.3k reinforces the bearish bias. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) remains the catalyst for assessing the central bank's response to trade-induced growth risks.

signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Intervention Risk
signals.text: JPY 1W momentum (+1.08%) and confirmed $34.5 billion intervention drive a sharp reversal against the 300bp US-JPY carry advantage. JPY COT net short -88.5k (30.6% long) creates short-squeeze risk (USD/JPY downside if shorts cover) if the pair breaks below Friday's 157.03 close. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) is the catalyst for determining if a rate hike will fundamentally support the yen's recovery.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk
signals.text: AUD COT net long +48.3k (74.8% long) creates a significant crowded-long unwind risk despite the RBA's 4.10% rate and hike bias. While retail sentiment is 90% short, the heavy speculative positioning suggests any shift in global risk appetite or commodity weakness triggers a sharp correction from 0.7208. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 5 May is the catalyst for confirming the hawkish trajectory.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Geopolitical Risk + Carry Disadvantage
signals.text: Trump's dissatisfaction with the Iran proposal and the resulting 150bp carry advantage for USD puts the 0.5900 level under immediate threat. NZD COT net short -16.8k aligns with the bearish technical close as geopolitical tensions favor the greenback's safe-haven status. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) on 27 May is the catalyst for determining if the hike bias can offset external headwinds.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning Overstretch
signals.text: GBP COT net long +32.6k (67.3% long) limits further upside potential despite the 5.01% UK 10Y yield advantage. The BoE's active hold signals risks to the current bullish momentum, especially as the pair faces resistance near 1.3575. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) is the catalyst for resolving the neutral carry spread against the Fed.
